title: "79% of Enterprises Are Confident They Can Scale AI Without Breaking Governance. Only 29% Can Even Find the Data. - GlobeNewswire"

summary_word_count: 309

description: "A recent survey reveals that while 79% of enterprises are confident in scaling AI, only 29% can locate the necessary data."
---

A new survey highlights a significant gap in enterprise readiness for scaling artificial intelligence (AI). While 79% of enterprises express confidence that they can scale AI initiatives without compromising governance, only 29% report being able to locate the data required for these efforts. This disparity raises critical questions about the actual capabilities of organizations in harnessing AI effectively, especially as the demand for data-driven decision-making continues to grow.

The findings suggest that many organizations may be overestimating their readiness to implement AI at scale. The survey indicates that while confidence is high, the practical challenges of data accessibility and governance remain daunting. This is particularly relevant in an era where data privacy and regulatory compliance are paramount. As enterprises push to integrate AI into their operations, the inability to find and manage data could hinder their progress and lead to governance issues, which could have long-term implications for trust and accountability.

In a competitive landscape where companies are racing to leverage AI for operational efficiency and innovation, the gap between confidence and capability could be a significant disadvantage. For instance, organizations that struggle with data governance may find themselves at risk of regulatory penalties or reputational damage. The survey underscores the need for a more robust data management strategy that not only facilitates AI deployment but also ensures compliance with evolving regulations. 

Moreover, the findings could have implications for technology vendors and service providers. Companies that offer solutions for data management and governance may see increased demand as enterprises seek to bridge this gap. As noted by [GlobeNewswire](https://news.google.?oc=5&hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US%3Aen), the disconnect between confidence and data accessibility could lead to a reevaluation of how enterprises approach AI governance and implementation.

Looking ahead, it will be crucial to monitor how organizations adapt their data strategies to align with their AI ambitions, particularly as regulatory pressures increase and the demand for transparency grows.
